source_lists.title: Cyberpunk
source_lists.desc:
Cyberpunk is a subgenre of science fiction in a future setting that tends to focus on the society of the proverbial "high tech low life"; featuring advanced technological and scientific achievements, such as information technology and cybernetics, juxtaposed with a degree of breakdown or radical change in the social order.
"Classic cyberpunk characters were marginalized, alienated loners who lived on the edge of society in generally dystopic futures where daily life was impacted by rapid technological change, an ubiquitous datasphere of computerized information, and invasive modification of the human body."
— Lawrence Person

source_lists.title: Cyberpunk
source_lists.desc:
Every Cyberpunk Movie ever made

Last Updated: 20 apr 2026
Something missing? Let me know, so I can add it to the list!

Cyberpunk is a sub-genre of sci-fi. At its core, it's about High Tech, Low Life. Often set in near-future settings where science has developed significant technological advancements, but not necessarily for the better. These technological advancements often lead to a fall in societal morale. The common folk often gets left behind technologically, despite the advancement of society as a whole, because of corporate greed, while the elite relish in their benefits. Causing bigger and bigger wealth inequality, this results in conflict between the have's and have-nots. The worlds of cyberpunk are often ruled by mega-corporations that wield… more

source_lists.title: Cyberpunk
source_lists.desc:
High tech low life, neon lights, androids and cyborgs, transhumanism and singularity, megacorps nestled in sterile skyscrapers, unchecked capitalism, class inequalities, cyberterrorism, urban decay - all that fun stuff. Here are the cyberpunk movies that I've seen, a.k.a the coolest and most relevant genre ever.

Some of these might be considered straight sci-fi by many, but I personally think that they have enough cyberpunk elements to be included here (or are at the very least cyberpunk-adjacent/tech-noirs/precursors to the genre). Some short films & shows included as well.

source_lists.title: Cyberpunk
source_lists.desc: Cyberpunk is a postmodern science fiction genre noted for its focus on high tech and low life. It features advanced science, such as information technology and cybernetics, coupled with a degree of breakdown or radical change in the social order.
